title: urumovia foliosa
intro: Urumovia foliosa, also known as the Mexican tea tree or Mexican mint, is a shrub-like plant native to Mexico and Central America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Urumovia foliosa include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs. Urumovia foliosa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
